article.title: Audiovisuel : Nawfel Raghay nommé DG de l'ARTI - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: L’Association des radios et télévisions indépendantes (ARTI) a annoncé, jeudi, la nomination de Nawfel Raghay en qualité de directeur général, marquant ainsi une nouvelle étape dans le processus de structuration de l’ARTI, qui entend renforcer sa capacité à suivre les mutations que connait le secteur de l’audiovisuel privé au Maroc. Cette nomination s’inscrit dans le […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: L’Association des radios et télévisions indépendantes (ARTI) a annoncé, jeudi, la nomination de Nawfel Raghay en qualité de directeur général, marquant ainsi une nouvelle étape dans le processus de structuration de l’ARTI, qui entend renforcer sa capacité à suivre les mutations que connait le secteur de l’audiovisuel privé au Maroc. Cette nomination s’inscrit dans le cadre des orientations adoptées par l’Assemblée générale du 23 janvier 2026 et du plan d’action 2026-2028 de l’Association, indique un communiqué de l’ARTI. L’écosystème médiatique traverse aujourd’hui une période de transformation profonde : mutation rapide des usages, pression croissante des plateformes numériques internationales, reconfiguration des modèles économiques et publicitaires, irruption de l’intelligence artificielle dans les pratiques de production et de diffusion, souligne l’ARTI. Ces défis imposent, selon l’ARTI, la consolidation de l’action collective des acteurs professionnels, le renforcement de leur capacité d’innovation et la défense de la place du secteur médiatique national dans un espace numérique et médiatique plus compétitif, poursuit-on.
article.summary: L’Association des radios et télévisions indépendantes (ARTI) a annoncé, jeudi, la nomination de Nawfel Raghay en qualité de directeur général, marquant ainsi une nouvelle étape dans le processus de structuration de l’ARTI, qui entend renforcer sa capacité à suivre les mutations que connait le secteur de l’audiovisuel privé au Maroc. Cette nomination s’inscrit dans le cadre des orientations adoptées par l’Assemblée générale du 23 janvier 2026 et du plan d’action 2026-2028 de l’Association, indiqu

article.title: Agadir : Le BHNS démarre - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: L’offre en services de transport se renforce à Agadir. La ville a mis en service, jeudi, sa première ligne du Bus à Haut Niveau de Service (BHNS), un projet structurant qui s’inscrit dans le cadre de programme de développement urbain de la capitale du Souss. Porté par la Société de développement local Agadir Mobilité, ce […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: L’offre en services de transport se renforce à Agadir. La ville a mis en service, jeudi, sa première ligne du Bus à Haut Niveau de Service (BHNS), un projet structurant qui s’inscrit dans le cadre de programme de développement urbain de la capitale du Souss. Porté par la Société de développement local Agadir Mobilité, ce projet baptisé “Amalway Trambus” est le fruit d’un partenariat institutionnel impliquant le ministère de l’Intérieur, le Conseil régional de Souss Massa, le Groupement des collectivités territoriales Grand Agadir pour le transport et la mobilité urbaine, la commune d’Agadir ainsi que plusieurs contributeurs publics et institutionnels. Plusieurs acteurs ont également contribué au montage financier de ce projet, notamment le Fonds d’accompagnement des réformes du transport (FRAT), le Conseil régional de Souss-Massa, la commune d’Agadir ainsi que l’Agence française de développement (AFD) qui soutient Agadir dans sa transition vers une mobilité durable et décarbonée. Cette première ligne s’étend sur un tracé reliant le Port d’Agadir au quartier de Tikiouine.
article.summary: L’offre en services de transport se renforce à Agadir. La ville a mis en service, jeudi, sa première ligne du Bus à Haut Niveau de Service (BHNS), un projet structurant qui s’inscrit dans le cadre de programme de développement urbain de la capitale du Souss.

article.title: Accompagnement des entrepreneurs : BoA lance Hsabati Pro - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: Bank Of Africa (BOA) a annoncé, mercredi, le lancement de Hsabati Pro, une plateforme digitale innovante dédiée à la gestion d’activité des entrepreneurs. Ce lancement intervient dans un environnement entrepreneurial en constante évolution, marqué par l’accélération des usages digitaux et des attentes croissantes en matière de simplicité, de réactivité et d’efficacité, souligne la banque, précisant […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: Bank Of Africa (BOA) a annoncé, mercredi, le lancement de Hsabati Pro, une plateforme digitale innovante dédiée à la gestion d’activité des entrepreneurs. Ce lancement intervient dans un environnement entrepreneurial en constante évolution, marqué par l’accélération des usages digitaux et des attentes croissantes en matière de simplicité, de réactivité et d’efficacité, souligne la banque, précisant que cette plateforme se présente comme une interface web centralisée permettant aux entrepreneurs de gérer l’intégralité de leur business en temps réel, précise la même source. HSABATI PRO couvre plusieurs fonctionnalités, notamment la facturation, la gestion de trésorerie, le suivi commercial (CRM), la gestion des stocks et des ressources humaines, et offre une visibilité immédiate sur les indicateurs clés de performance. En automatisant les tâches administratives récurrentes, BOA permet aux chefs d’entreprise de se libérer des contraintes logistiques pour se concentrer pleinement sur le développement de leur activité.
article.summary: Bank Of Africa (BOA) a annoncé, mercredi, le lancement de Hsabati Pro, une plateforme digitale innovante dédiée à la gestion d’activité des entrepreneurs. Ce lancement intervient dans un environnement entrepreneurial en constante évolution, marqué par l’accélération des usages digitaux et des attentes croissantes en matière de simplicité, de réactivité et d’efficacité, souligne la banque, précisant que cette plateforme se présente comme une interface web centralisée permettant aux entrepreneurs

article.title: Education : Un budget en hausse de 68% depuis 2021 - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: Le budget alloué au secteur de l’éducation a connu une forte croissance sur la période 2021-2026. Il est ainsi passé de 58,9 milliards de DH en 2021 à un total de 99,1 milliards de DH en 2026, soit une augmentation de 68%. Au total, 788 établissements scolaires ont été créés, dont 472 en milieu rural, […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: Le budget alloué au secteur de l’éducation a connu une forte croissance sur la période 2021-2026. Il est ainsi passé de 58,9 milliards de DH en 2021 à un total de 99,1 milliards de DH en 2026, soit une augmentation de 68%. Au total, 788 établissements scolaires ont été créés, dont 472 en milieu rural, 109 écoles communautaires et 120 internats, selon un document du gouvernement sur les projets sociaux. Concernant l’élargissement de l’offre universitaire, quelques 15 établissements universitaires publics ont été ouverts, dont 5 ont été nouvellement créés durant cette période, comprenant trois Facultés de Médecine et de Pharmacie à Béni Mellal, Errachidia et Guelmim, ainsi que deux Écoles nationales d’intelligence artificielle et du numérique à Berkane et Taroudant.
article.summary: Le budget alloué au secteur de l’éducation a connu une forte croissance sur la période 2021-2026. Il est ainsi passé de 58,9 milliards de DH en 2021 à un total de 99,1 milliards de DH en 2026, soit une augmentation de 68%.

article.title: Santé : Le gouvernement dresse le bilan - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: Le gouvernement a fait le point sur la mise en œuvre des réformes structurelles et de programmes sociaux en vue d’étendre la protection sociale et à renforcer l’accès équitable aux services de base, conformément aux Hautes Orientations Royales. Dans le domaine de la santé, le budget est ainsi passé de 19,7 milliards de DH en […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: Le gouvernement a fait le point sur la mise en œuvre des réformes structurelles et de programmes sociaux en vue d’étendre la protection sociale et à renforcer l’accès équitable aux services de base, conformément aux Hautes Orientations Royales. Dans le domaine de la santé, le budget est ainsi passé de 19,7 milliards de DH en 2021 à 42,4 milliards de DH en 2026, soit une progression de 115% au cours du présent mandat, indique un document sur le bilan gouvernemental. Sur cette période, trois nouveaux CHU ont été réalisés à Tanger, Agadir et Rabat, parallèlement au lancement des travaux de construction de cinq nouveaux CHU pour un montant global avoisinant 17,2 milliards de DH à Laâyoune, Rabat, Guelmim, Berrechid et Béni Mellal. A cela s’ajoute des rénovations qui ont concernés cinq hôpitaux universitaires à Fès, Casablanca, Rabat, Marrakech et Oujda, pour un budget de 1,7 milliard de DH. De plus, 29 projets hospitaliers ont été réalisés aux niveaux régional et provincial sur la période 2022-2025, permettant d’accroître la capacité litière nationale de 3.168 lits.
article.summary: Le gouvernement a fait le point sur la mise en œuvre des réformes structurelles et de programmes sociaux en vue d’étendre la protection sociale et à renforcer l’accès équitable aux services de base, conformément aux Hautes Orientations Royales. Dans le domaine de la santé, le budget est ainsi passé de 19,7 milliards de DH en 2021 à 42,4 milliards de DH en 2026, soit une progression de 115% au cours du présent mandat, indique un document sur le bilan gouvernemental.

article.title: Maroc-Norvège : Accord pour la mise en œuvre de l’Article 6.2 de l’Accord de Paris - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: Le Maroc et la Norvège ont signé mardi dernier un accord visant à établir un cadre de coopération pour le développement d’approches collaboratives fondées sur les mécanismes de marché prévus par l’Article 6.2 de l’Accord de Paris sur le Climat. Cet accord a été signé par la ministre de la Transition énergétique et du Développement […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: Le Maroc et la Norvège ont signé mardi dernier un accord visant à établir un cadre de coopération pour le développement d’approches collaboratives fondées sur les mécanismes de marché prévus par l’Article 6.2 de l’Accord de Paris sur le Climat. Cet accord a été signé par la ministre de la Transition énergétique et du Développement durable, Leila Benali, et le ministre norvégien du Climat et de l’Environnement, Andreas Bjelland Eriksen, lors d’une cérémonie tenue à distance, en présence de l’ambassadrice du Maroc en Norvège, Nabila Freidji, et de l’ambassadeur du Norvège au Maroc, Helge Seland. Il permet la mise en œuvre de projets conjoints générant des résultats d’atténuation transférés au niveau international (ITMOs), contribuant ainsi à l’atteinte des Contributions Déterminées au niveau national des deux pays, indique un communiqué du ministère de la Transition énergétique et du Développement durable.
article.summary: Le Maroc et la Norvège ont signé mardi dernier un accord visant à établir un cadre de coopération pour le développement d’approches collaboratives fondées sur les mécanismes de marché prévus par l’Article 6.2 de l’Accord de Paris sur le Climat. article.title: Ain Diab accueille la 1re étape 2026 des Junior Qualifying Series pour l'Afrique et l'Europe - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: La saison 2026 des Junior Qualifying Series (JQS) Europe et Afrique de la World Surf League (WSL) débute du 6 au 10 mai à Aïn Diab, Casablanca, avec le Morocco Mall Junior Pro Casablanca. Cette première étape réunit 128 hommes et 56 femmes de près de 15 nationalités. Le Maroc sera représenté par 9 surfeurs […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: La saison 2026 des Junior Qualifying Series (JQS) Europe et Afrique de la World Surf League (WSL) débute du 6 au 10 mai à Aïn Diab, Casablanca, avec le Morocco Mall Junior Pro Casablanca. Cette première étape réunit 128 hommes et 56 femmes de près de 15 nationalités. Le Maroc sera représenté par 9 surfeurs (5 hommes et 4 femmes), illustrant l’essor du surf national. Parmi les favoris figurent les Espagnols Carla Morera De La Vall et Alfonso Suarez, champion junior européen 2025. Côté africain, les Sud-Africains Ben Esterhuyse et Levi Epenetos sont attendus parmi les prétendants.
article.summary: La saison 2026 des Junior Qualifying Series (JQS) Europe et Afrique de la World Surf League (WSL) débute du 6 au 10 mai à Aïn Diab, Casablanca, avec le Morocco Mall Junior Pro Casablanca. Cette première étape réunit 128 hommes et 56 femmes de près de 15 nationalités.

article.title: Aleph Hospitality : Abdellah Essonni nommé VP pour l'Afrique du Nord - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: Aleph Hospitality, la première et la plus grande société indépendante de gestion hôtelière au Moyen-Orient et en Afrique, poursuit sa stratégie de croissance dans la région. Elle vient d’ouvrir son bureau régional à Casablanca, placé sous la direction d’Abdellah Essonni, Vice-Président Régional pour l’Afrique du Nord. Au total, Aleph Hospitality prévoit l’ouverture de quatre nouveaux […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: Aleph Hospitality, la première et la plus grande société indépendante de gestion hôtelière au Moyen-Orient et en Afrique, poursuit sa stratégie de croissance dans la région. Elle vient d’ouvrir son bureau régional à Casablanca, placé sous la direction d’Abdellah Essonni, Vice-Président Régional pour l’Afrique du Nord. Au total, Aleph Hospitality prévoit l’ouverture de quatre nouveaux bureaux régionaux en 2026, afin de renforcer son expertise régionale et sa capacité de réponse aux besoins des propriétaires hôteliers. L’inauguration du bureau de Casablanca constitue une étape clé de ce déploiement et servira de hub stratégique pour les opérations en pleine croissance du groupe en Afrique du Nord. L’entreprise prévoit également de renforcer ses équipes au Maroc au cours de l’année, indique la société. En tant que principal représentant du groupe dans la région, Abdellah Essonni aura pour mission de piloter les activités d’Aleph Hospitality en Afrique du Nord. Fort de plus de 35 ans d’expérience dans la gestion hôtelière, il a précédemment occupé les fonctions de Dg de Saudi Hospitality Company (filiale du PIF) ainsi que de Dg d’Abjar Hotels International à Dubaï.
article.summary: Aleph Hospitality, la première et la plus grande société indépendante de gestion hôtelière au Moyen-Orient et en Afrique, poursuit sa stratégie de croissance dans la région. Elle vient d’ouvrir son bureau régional à Casablanca, placé sous la direction d’Abdellah Essonni, Vice-Président Régional pour l’Afrique du Nord.

article.title: Gitex Future Health : Des innovateurs de la santé et des créateurs de solutions d’IA distingués - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: Des innovateurs de la santé et des créateurs de solutions d’IA ont été primés lors du Gitex Future Health Africa Morocco 2026 organisé du 4 au 6 mai à Casablanca. La cérémonie de remise des prix a réuni de jeunes entreprises développant des technologies avancées dans des domaines aussi stratégiques que la santé digitale, le […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: Des innovateurs de la santé et des créateurs de solutions d’IA ont été primés lors du Gitex Future Health Africa Morocco 2026 organisé du 4 au 6 mai à Casablanca. La cérémonie de remise des prix a réuni de jeunes entreprises développant des technologies avancées dans des domaines aussi stratégiques que la santé digitale, le diagnostic par IA, les soins préventifs, les plateformes de données et l’innovation en santé centrée sur le patient. La journée de clôture a été rythmée par les finales et la cérémonie de remise des prix de la compétition internationale de pitch dédiée aux startups, le Supernova Challenge, qui distingue des solutions de santé à fort impact. Blink Pharma, startup marocaine, a ainsi remporté la première place lors de la cérémonie de remise des prix du Supernova Challenge, confirmant ainsi la vitalité de l’innovation marocaine dans la healthtech au sein d’un écosystème continental en pleine structuration. La veille, le LovAfrica Buildathon s’était conclu à l’issue d’un défi collaboratif intensif consacré à l’identification de besoins de santé encore insuffisamment visibles, grâce à l’intelligence artificielle et à des technologies digitales accessibles.
article.summary: Des innovateurs de la santé et des créateurs de solutions d’IA ont été primés lors du Gitex Future Health Africa Morocco 2026 organisé du 4 au 6 mai à Casablanca. La cérémonie de remise des prix a réuni de jeunes entreprises développant des technologies avancées dans des domaines aussi stratégiques que la santé digitale, le diagnostic par IA, les soins préventifs, les plateformes de données et l’innovation en santé centrée sur le patient.

article.title: Rabat: Abdelhak Azzouzi dévoile une ambitieuse traduction-exégèse du Coran au SIEL - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: À l’occasion du Salon International de l’Édition et du Livre (SIEL), organisé le 6 mai à Rabat, Abdelhak Azzouzi a présenté une œuvre éditoriale d’envergure consacrée à la traduction et à l’exégèse du Coran en langue française. Une publication qui ambitionne de renouveler l’approche francophone du texte sacré, en conciliant rigueur académique, profondeur spirituelle et […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: À l’occasion du Salon International de l’Édition et du Livre (SIEL), organisé le 6 mai à Rabat, Abdelhak Azzouzi a présenté une œuvre éditoriale d’envergure consacrée à la traduction et à l’exégèse du Coran en langue française. Une publication qui ambitionne de renouveler l’approche francophone du texte sacré, en conciliant rigueur académique, profondeur spirituelle et ouverture sur les enjeux contemporains. Figure reconnue de la pensée islamique contemporaine et des relations internationales, le président de la Chaire des Nations Unies pour l’Alliance des Civilisations et membre du conseil d’administration de Université Euromed de Fès a présenté un travail édité par les éditions internationales Al-Bouraq, au terme de plus de dix années de recherche et de rédaction. La rencontre, animée par Abderrahman Tenkoul, a réuni un public composé d’universitaires, d’étudiants et d’acteurs du monde culturel. Structurée en trois volumes totalisant plus de 3.200 pages, cette traduction-exégèse dépasse le simple exercice de transposition linguistique. L’auteur propose une lecture analytique et contextualisée du texte coranique, fondée sur une approche à la fois heuristique et herméneutique.
article.summary: À l’occasion du Salon International de l’Édition et du Livre (SIEL), organisé le 6 mai à Rabat, Abdelhak Azzouzi a présenté une œuvre éditoriale d’envergure consacrée à la traduction et à l’exégèse du Coran en langue française. Une publication qui ambitionne de renouveler l’approche francophone du texte sacré, en conciliant rigueur académique, profondeur spirituelle et ouverture sur les enjeux contemporains.

article.title: Le TIIC revient pour une 4e édition - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: Après trois éditions réussies, la Fondation Al Mada et Caz’impro annoncent la 4ᵉ édition du Tournoi International d’Improvisation de Casablanca (TIIC), qui aura lieu les 8 et 9 mai 2026 à la Villa des Arts de Casablanca. Cette édition marque une étape majeure avec la participation, pour la première fois, d’équipes professionnelles venues du Maroc, […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: Après trois éditions réussies, la Fondation Al Mada et Caz’impro annoncent la 4ᵉ édition du Tournoi International d’Improvisation de Casablanca (TIIC), qui aura lieu les 8 et 9 mai 2026 à la Villa des Arts de Casablanca. Cette édition marque une étape majeure avec la participation, pour la première fois, d’équipes professionnelles venues du Maroc, de Belgique, de France et de Suisse, dont certaines ont pris part à la dernière Coupe du Monde d’improvisation en Belgique. Au programme : des matchs d’improvisation sur des thèmes imposés, arbitrés par des professionnels indépendants, avec un public appelé à voter pour désigner les vainqueurs. Grande nouveauté cette année : une scène circulaire inspirée des patinoires canadiennes d’impro, offrant une immersion renforcée et une proximité inédite entre artistes et spectateurs.
article.summary: Après trois éditions réussies, la Fondation Al Mada et Caz’impro annoncent la 4ᵉ édition du Tournoi International d’Improvisation de Casablanca (TIIC), qui aura lieu les 8 et 9 mai 2026 à la Villa des Arts de Casablanca. Cette édition marque une étape majeure avec la participation, pour la première fois, d’équipes professionnelles venues du Maroc, de Belgique, de France et de Suisse, dont certaines ont pris part à la dernière Coupe du Monde d’improvisation en Belgique.

article.title: JO 2028 : Des bourses olympiques pour 23 athlètes - L'Economiste
article.subtitle: Le Comité National Olympique Marocain (CNOM) a réuni des athlètes récemment à Bouznika pour la préparation des Jeux Olympiques de Los Angeles 2028. Cette rencontre a constitué une étape importante dans le déploiement du programme des Bourses olympiques, en réunissant les sportifs concernés autour d’une dynamique commune de préparation et d’accompagnement vers l’échéance olympique de […]
article.author: L'Economiste
article.content: Le Comité National Olympique Marocain (CNOM) a réuni des athlètes récemment à Bouznika pour la préparation des Jeux Olympiques de Los Angeles 2028. Cette rencontre a constitué une étape importante dans le déploiement du programme des Bourses olympiques, en réunissant les sportifs concernés autour d’une dynamique commune de préparation et d’accompagnement vers l’échéance olympique de Los Angeles 2028, indique le CNOM. Au total, 23 athlètes vont bénéficier de ces bourses. Cette rencontre a permis de présenter les grandes orientations du programme, les modalités de suivi, les outils d’accompagnement mobilisés, ainsi que les critères de sélection et de catégorisation des boursiers et les dispositifs mis en place pour optimiser la préparation des sportifs marocains. Le programme repose sur une approche globale visant à renforcer les conditions de préparation de l’élite sportive nationale à travers plusieurs leviers complémentaires, incluant l’accompagnement sportif, médical, mental, logistique et scientifique, explique le CNOM. Les 23 athlètes concernés à ce jour sont issus de 8 fédérations sportives.
article.summary: Le Comité National Olympique Marocain (CNOM) a réuni des athlètes récemment à Bouznika pour la préparation des Jeux Olympiques de Los Angeles 2028. Cette rencontre a constitué une étape importante dans le déploiement du programme des Bourses olympiques, en réunissant les sportifs concernés autour d’une dynamique commune de préparation et d’accompagnement vers l’échéance olympique de Los Angeles 2028, indique le CNOM.

article.title: Peugeot: un cas de réhabilitation d'image
article.subtitle: L’image de Peugeot a été changée de fond en comble, en quelques années, en dosant l’implantation commerciale, notamment le service après vente, et les campagnes de publicité ou de relations publiques. La SOPRIAM, licenciée Peugeot (et Citroën) a presque doublé ses ventes entre 1987 et 1991 en passant de 4.855 à 9.509 véhicules. Les deux […]

article.summary: L’image de Peugeot a été changée de fond en comble, en quelques années, en dosant l’implantation commerciale, notamment le service après vente, et les campagnes de publicité ou de relations publiques. La SOPRIAM, licenciée Peugeot (et Citroën) a presque doublé ses ventes entre 1987 et 1991 en passant de 4.855 à 9.509 véhicules.

article.title: Transports : réaménagements, extension, sur tous les aéroports
article.subtitle: Nos aéroports n’ont d’international que la longueur de leurs pistes. Leurs bâtiments sont petits, peu fonctionnels, sans manque de prestige. article.title: Do Wall Street Analysts Like Blackstone Stock?
article.subtitle: Blackstone has underperformed the broader market over the past year, and analysts remain cautiously optimistic about the stock’s prospects.
article.author: Aritra Gangopadhyay
article.content: New York-based Blackstone Inc. (BX) is an alternative asset management firm specializing in private equity, venture capital, real estate, hedge fund solutions, credit, secondary funds of funds, public debt and equity and multi-asset class strategies. The company has a market cap of $91.4 billion and invests in early-stage, seed, middle-market, mature, late-stage venture, growth capital, emerging-growth, turnaround, and later-stage companies. Shares of the company have lagged behind the broader market over the past year and in 2026. BX stock has declined 9.8% over the past 52 weeks and 20.4% on a YTD basis. In comparison, the S&P 500 Index ($SPX) has returned 28.5% over the past year and risen 6% in 2026. Narrowing the focus, BX has also underperformed the State Street Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLF), which rose 4.4% over the past 52 weeks and its 5.8% decrease this year. On Apr. 23, BX stock declined marginally following the release of its Q1 2026 earnings. The company’s revenue amounted to $3.6 billion and surpassed the Street’s estimates. Moreover, its adjusted EPS came in at $1.36 and also surpassed Wall Street’s forecasts.
article.summary: New York-based Blackstone Inc. (BX) is an alternative asset management firm specializing in private equity, venture capital, real estate, hedge fund solutions, credit, secondary funds of funds, public debt and equity and multi-asset class strategies.

article.title: SpaceX isn't public yet, but Wall Street is already building ETFs for it
article.subtitle: SpaceX's private market price has surged nearly 700% since 2023, giving it an estimated valuation near $1.5 trillion — enough to make the ETF world a reason ...
article.author: Jared Blikre
article.content: ) isn’t public yet, but Wall Street is already preparing for blastoff. but has not listed yet. Its private market price has surged nearly 700% since 2023, giving it an estimated valuation near $1.5 trillion — enough to make the ETF world a reason to move before the listing. Todd Sohn, chief ETF strategist at Strategas, noted that nine space-related ETFs have been launched or filed over the past three months. One existing space ETF index is also changing its rules so that a company like SpaceX could become eligible as early as the close of its first day of exchange trading after an IPO. The race isn’t only about rockets. It’s also about how quickly Wall Street can package private market giants once they hit public markets. Historically, new listings had to wait before joining major indexes. The Nasdaq 100 ( ) used to require three full months of trading, while S&P 500 ( Nasdaq has adopted a fast-entry path that could allow a massive new listing into the Nasdaq 100 after only 15 trading days if it clears the size test. The S&P 500 is moving more slowly, with S&P Dow Jones Indices considering a shorter six-month waiting period for some megacap IPOs.
article.summary: ) isn’t public yet, but Wall Street is already preparing for blastoff. but has not listed yet.

article.title: Box CEO Aaron Levie Says 'Each Engineer Is 2X Or 5X More Capable' As AI Fuels Hiring Surge Instead Of Job Cuts
article.subtitle: Box Inc. CEO Aaron Levie on Saturday said artificial intelligence (AI) is increasing engineer productivity and is more likely to drive hiring growth across...
article.author: Snigdha Gairola
article.content: on Saturday said artificial intelligence (AI) is increasing engineer productivity and is more likely to drive hiring growth across industries than reduce the need for software engineers. , Levie challenged the idea that AI will reduce demand for software engineers, outlining a scenario in which businesses historically constrained by cost and talent shortages now gain access to advanced AI tools. "If you think AI replaces software engineers, here's a quick thought experiment," Levie wrote, describing how companies in industries like life sciences previously scaled back software ambitions due to limited engineering resources. "Every new software project has a fixed cost of a certain-sized team," he said, noting that this forced organizations to prioritize only essential initiatives. With AI tools now widely available, Levie said engineers across industries are using "the same AI models as the tech industry," effectively boosting productivity and narrowing the gap between tech companies and traditional sectors. "Do you continue with your pared-down approach, or do you start to hire more engineers because each engineer is 2X or 5X more capable than before?" he wrote.
article.summary: on Saturday said artificial intelligence (AI) is increasing engineer productivity and is more likely to drive hiring growth across industries than reduce the need for software engineers. , Levie challenged the idea that AI will reduce demand for software engineers, outlining a scenario in which businesses historically constrained by cost and talent shortages now gain access to advanced AI tools.

article.title: Analysis-SpaceX IPO gives Musk sweeping power and curbs shareholder rights
article.subtitle: SpaceX has adopted corporate governance policies that will erode typical shareholder protections in unprecedented ways, giving founder Elon Musk virtually...
article.author: Anirban Sen, Ross Kerber and Suzanne McGee
article.content: By Anirban Sen, Ross Kerber and Suzanne McGee NEW YORK, May 6 (Reuters) - SpaceX has adopted corporate governance policies that will erode typical shareholder protections in unprecedented ways, giving founder Elon Musk virtually unchecked executive authority when the rocket maker goes public later this year. Excerpts of SpaceX's IPO registration statement reviewed by Reuters show the company is combining supervoting shares, mandatory arbitration, stricter rules on shareholder proposals and Texas corporate law to give Musk and other insiders broad control. At the same time, it sharply limits investors' ability to challenge ‌management, sue in court and force votes on governance issues. And the only person who can fire Musk is Musk, who will retain majority control through supervoting shares. “It closes the voting door, the courthouse door and the proposal door simultaneously. It’s unprecedented in terms of creating a total lack ‌of accountability,” said Bruce Herbert, CEO of Seattle-based sustainability-focused wealth management firm Newground Social Investment, which challenged Musk at his electric-vehicle company, , with a shareholder proposal that won 49% of the vote in November.
article.summary: By Anirban Sen, Ross Kerber and Suzanne McGee NEW YORK, May 6 (Reuters) - SpaceX has adopted corporate governance policies that will erode typical shareholder protections in unprecedented ways, giving founder Elon Musk virtually unchecked executive authority when the rocket maker goes public later this year. What your dollar is going to be able to buy is going to shrink." — and no, you don't have to deal with tenants or fix freezers. A weaker dollar makes imports more expensive, which pushes up the prices of everything imported. Food, fuel and so on. This compounds with affordability issues that've already been felt, such as the inflationary effects of rising oil costs. The greenback has fallen in recent history as well, with a "strange sell-off" that saw the U.S. dollar drop by 9% at the beginning of 2025 (2).

article.title: Uber forecasts strong second-quarter bookings despite Middle East woes

article.author: Akash Sriram
article.content: May 6 (Reuters) - Uber Technologies forecast second-quarter bookings above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday on strong demand for ride-hailing and delivery services, even though ‌the Middle East conflict weighed on its growth. Shares of the San Francisco-based company ‌rose about 7%. The strong forecast is a sign that the company's strategy of keeping prices steady while pushing into ​higher-margin areas such as its platform for businesses are paying off, helping it navigate higher fuel costs and geopolitical tensions. Strong delivery demand in international markets, including Australia, and expansion into new geographies such as Denmark have also helped power growth. "I see it as a sign of durable demand, ‌plus the significant runway still ⁠left for Uber gain market share in US suburbs and international markets," said Adam Ballantyne, senior analyst at shareholder Cambiar Investors, referring to the ⁠upbeat forecast. Uber said it expects gross bookings of $56.25 billion to $57.75 billion for the June quarter, above analysts' average estimate of $56.07 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG. It factors in a roughly 60 basis-point ​drag from ​the Middle East conflict.
article.summary: May 6 (Reuters) - Uber Technologies forecast second-quarter bookings above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday on strong demand for ride-hailing and delivery services, even though ‌the Middle East conflict weighed on its growth. article.title: 3 charts explain how the Iran war oil shock could impact the economy
article.subtitle: Deutsche Bank explains the economic impacts of oil shocks in three charts.
article.author: Jake Conley
article.content: The US has spent the past two decades insulating itself from oil price shocks, Deutsche Bank chief US economist Matthew Luzzetti wrote in a recent note to clients. But as three charts from Luzzetti show, the current oil shock from the war in Iran may still have rippling economic effects. The US is the leading exporter of crude oil, though the commodity is globally priced, and events in one corner of the globe are felt worldwide. Over the past nine weeks, the as the world’s largest exporter of the bedrock energy product, according to Bloomberg, as data from the Energy Information Administration shows weekly exports have That said, the US is not truly energy independent. As the chart below shows, while the US is a net exporter of petroleum products generally, a category that includes crude oil and refined products, it is a net importer of the underlying crude oil. That’s because while US shale oil is primarily light, sweet crude, the refinery complex along the US’s Gulf Coast is designed primarily to handle heavy crude imported from Canada and a few other countries.
article.summary: The US has spent the past two decades insulating itself from oil price shocks, Deutsche Bank chief US economist Matthew Luzzetti wrote in a recent note to clients. But as three charts from Luzzetti show, the current oil shock from the war in Iran may still have rippling economic effects.

article.title: Positioning for a Big Move: SBUX Long Straddle Trade Idea
article.subtitle: When volatility is low, options become cheaper, so today we’re looking for stocks with a low IV Percentile which could be good candidates for a Long Straddle...
article.author: Gavin McMaster
article.content: Volatility is back towards the lowest levels we have seen in 2025 with the VIX Index closing at 17.38 on Tuesday. When volatility is low, options become cheaper, so today we’re looking for stocks with a low IV Percentile which could be good candidates for a Long Straddle trade. First, let’s find stock with a low IV Percentile using the Stock Screener and the following parameters: Starbucks (SBUX) stands out as a good candidate for a long straddle trade. A long straddle is an advanced options strategy used when a trader is seeking to profit from a big move in either direction and / or an increase in implied volatility. To execute the strategy, a trader would buy a call and a put with the following conditions: Both options must use the same underlying stock Both options must have the same expiration Both options must have the same strike price Since it involves having to buy both a call and a put, the trader must pay two premiums up-front, which also happens to be the maximum possible loss. The potential profit is theoretically unlimited, although the trade will lose money each day through time decay if a big move does not occur.
article.summary: Volatility is back towards the lowest levels we have seen in 2025 with the VIX Index closing at 17.38 on Tuesday. When volatility is low, options become cheaper, so today we’re looking for stocks with a low IV Percentile which could be good candidates for a Long Straddle trade.

article.title: Oil prices deepen losses as Trump points to potential Iran agreement
article.subtitle: Oil prices continued to fall on Wednesday after U. S.
article.author: Fiona Craig
article.content: Oil prices continued to fall on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump suggested that negotiations with Iran were making progress, raising expectations that disrupted Middle Eastern oil supply could eventually return to global markets. Brent crude futures dropped $6.70, or 6.1%, to $103.17 a barrel by 08:56 GMT after earlier touching their lowest level in nearly two weeks. U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude declined $6.77, or 6.6%, to $95.50 a barrel. Both benchmarks had already fallen around 4% in the previous session. The suspension of marine traffic through the Strait of Hormuz since February has significantly tightened global supply conditions and pushed oil prices higher, with Brent crude reaching its strongest level since March 2022 last week. However, Trump said Tuesday that he would temporarily suspend the U.S. operation escorting ships through the strategic waterway, citing progress toward a broader agreement with Iran. While the president did not provide further details, he said the U.S. Navy would continue enforcing its blockade on Iranian ports.
article.summary: Oil prices continued to fall on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump suggested that negotiations with Iran were making progress, raising expectations that disrupted Middle Eastern oil supply could eventually return to global markets.

article.title: Up 75% Already in 2026, Korea’s Stock Market Is Hotter Than Ever
article.subtitle: (Bloomberg) -- South Korean stocks are on the cusp of surpassing last year’s world-beating 76% gain, even though 2026 is less than five months old.Most Read ...
article.author: Sangmi Cha
article.content: (Bloomberg) -- South Korean stocks are on the cusp of surpassing last year’s world-beating 76% gain, even though 2026 is less than five months old. The benchmark Kospi surged 6.5% Wednesday to bring this year’s advance to 75%, its eighth daily gain of more than 5% this year, compared with just one such rally in 2025. Overseas investors bought more than $2 billion of Kospi shares for the day, just a fraction below the record seen in October. All of this enthusiasm boils down to one theme: artificial intelligence. Long disregarded by global investors, Korean companies are now enjoying a re-rating as their technological edge across the AI supply chain makes their products — and stocks — highly sought after. “There’s still massive upside,” said Chan H. Lee, a managing partner at hedge fund Petra Capital Management in Seoul. “This isn’t just a local Korea story; it’s a global AI memory cycle. Samsung Electronics’ intrinsic value is finally being recognized.” Memory chipmakers are among the most sought after stocks in the world due to a supply squeeze and expectations they can keep growing regardless of the overall economic cycle. Even after Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.
article.summary: (Bloomberg) -- South Korean stocks are on the cusp of surpassing last year’s world-beating 76% gain, even though 2026 is less than five months old. article.author: Reuters
article.content: KUALA LUMPUR, May 6 (Reuters) - French energy technology firm Schneider Electric plans to open a Southeast Asian training centre in ‌Malaysia this year, a senior executive said on Wednesday, as ‌a boom in infrastructure drives up power demand in the region. Southeast Asia's ​data centre capacity is expected to grow threefold by 2030, according to analysts, with Malaysia emerging as a major hotspot, attracting investments from tech giants such as , Amazon and Alphabet's Google in recent years. The ‌country is also a ⁠key hub for semiconductors, accounting for about 13% of global testing and packaging. AI demand is expected to drive ⁠growth in both sectors, putting pressure on firms to improve energy efficiency, the president of Schneider Electric's semiconductors and battery segment Henri Berthe told ​Reuters. "This growth ​is pushing us to invest in ​Southeast Asia," he said on ‌the sidelines of a regional industry forum. Berthe said the training centre would provide technical skills to the company's partners and end users throughout the region, including semiconductor firms, using operational equipment ranging from medium‑voltage energy management systems to data centre solutions.

article.title: A new Trump executive order could reshape retirement savings — who qualifies and when changes begin
article.subtitle: Around 56 million Americans lack a workplace retirement plan, but TrumpIRA.gov aims to connect them to IRAs and up to $1,000 in federal matching funds.
article.author: Clay Halton
article.content: For millions of Americans, saving for retirement isn't just difficult, it's out of reach. Roughly 56 million workers don't have access to a 401(k) or other employer-sponsored retirement plan, according to research from The Pew Charitable Trusts (1). Without those tools, building long-term savings becomes far less likely. Last week, President Donald Trump signed a new executive order aimed at closing that gap (2). The order directs the Treasury Department to build a new federal website where workers without access to employer-sponsored retirement plans can compare and enroll in individual retirement accounts and, if eligible, receive a government savings match. — and no, you don't have to deal with tenants or fix freezers. Here's how and begs investors not to miss this ‘explosion’ Dave Ramsey warns nearly 50% of Americans are making 1 big Social Security mistake — The changes could expand access to retirement tools for millions. But how much they ultimately help will depend on who signs up and what happens next in Congress. Here's who qualifies, when the changes begin and what it could mean for your money.
article.summary: For millions of Americans, saving for retirement isn't just difficult, it's out of reach. Roughly 56 million workers don't have access to a 401(k) or other employer-sponsored retirement plan, according to research from The Pew Charitable Trusts (1).

article.title: Positioning for the Quantum Future: How Delta Gold Technologies Is Building Early Advantage

article.content: Quantum technology is still in its early innings, but the companies laying the groundwork today, especially those securing key intellectual property, could define the market for decades to come. Delta Gold Technologies plc (USOTC:DGQTF) (AQSE:DGQ) is making a strategic bet that getting ahead now will pay off later. In a recent conversation, CEO Michael Jones highlighted a major step forward: the company’s provisional patent filing for quantum transducer structures. Beyond the technical achievement, the timing stands out, Delta Gold reached this milestone roughly six months ahead of schedule. In a fast-moving, high-stakes field like quantum, that kind of execution signals both focus and momentum. A core part of the company’s strategy is its close collaboration with leading research institutions. By working with the University of Toronto and Penn State, Delta Gold is tapping into world-class academic expertise while guiding research toward practical, commercial outcomes. This model, aligning academic discovery with industry application, has historically been a powerful driver of innovation in the United States and beyond.
article.summary: Quantum technology is still in its early innings, but the companies laying the groundwork today, especially those securing key intellectual property, could define the market for decades to come. Delta Gold Technologies plc (USOTC:DGQTF) (AQSE:DGQ) is making a strategic bet that getting ahead now will pay off later.

Moving money across borders is often slow, expensive, and tied to banking hours. Stablecoins offer a version of digital dollars that can move at any time. The company says payments to valid claimants will begin in May 2026. If you were covered by a Blue Cross or Blue Shield health insurance policy between 2008 and 2020 — and filed a settlement claim prior to Nov. 5, 2021 — you may be eligible to receive a settlement payment. The $2.67 billion settlement fund, now estimated at $1.

See our Thanks to elevated home prices, many homeowners have found themselves with an unexpected boost in their home equity in recent years. That creates an opportunity to borrow with a home equity line of credit (HELOC). Whether you'll be approved for a HELOC will depend on , mortgage balance, debt-to-income ratio, and credit score, among other factors. that is a revolving credit line secured by your home equity. Your home equity is the difference between your home's value and your mortgage balance — or the portion of the house that you actually own. With a HELOC, you can turn a portion of your into a line of credit, withdrawing as much or as little as you want up to your credit limit. You can then repay — and re-borrow — from the HELOC, much like a credit card, until the HELOC’s draw period is up. Draw periods usually last 10 years, and during that time, you’ll typically make interest-only payments based on the .

article.title: What Long-Term Investors Should Know Before Buying This Broad Market ETF
article.subtitle: Just because a fund holds hundreds or even thousands of stocks doesn't mean it's diversified.
article.author: David Dierking
article.content: Just because a fund holds hundreds or even thousands of stocks doesn't mean it's diversified. owns roughly 3,500 U.S. stocks. That sounds like strong The problem exists with the top-heavy concentration at the top of the portfolio. The fund's top three holdings are . Combined, they account for 17% of the fund's assets. Top-10 holdings account for 32%. That's a lot of influence from just a handful of stocks. This is a consistent problem that occurs in almost any exchange-traded fund (ETF) that weights its holdings by market cap. In any , between 30% and 40% of the portfolio is in less than a dozen stocks. has nearly 50% of its portfolio in just five stocks: has 39% of its portfolio in just two stocks: True diversification doesn't come from the number of stocks in a portfolio. It depends on the weights of those components and how much idiosyncratic risk a fund exposes its shareholders to. If you're investing in a total market fund, odds are you're making a larger bet on fewer stocks. It's best to understand this before you dive in. *Average returns of all recommendations since inception. Cost basis and return based on previous market day close. Invest better with The Motley Fool.

After breaking out late last summer, MAGS ran up to its late-October peak. Then it spent about six months stuck below its highs and nearly crossed into bear market territory during the Iran war sell-off. That flips the “Magnificent Seven” from drag to driver. The group had been weighing on the market for months. But megacap stocks began leading again off the March 30 lows as war headlines cooled and investors moved back into the AI trade, including the ) was the first to reclaim a record close in April. Alphabet ( ) quickly followed, with Alphabet standing out as the clear trend bucker. It kept rallying through much of the broader Magnificent Seven stall and is now up far more than the group over the past year — over 140% versus 45%. The stock had lagged other megacap winners for much of the bull market that began off the October 2022 lows.

article.subtitle: Software developer Rave on Thursday filed an antitrust lawsuit against Apple, alleging that the iPhone maker ‌kicked Rave's app for shared viewing of video...
article.author: Stephen Nellis
article.content: SAN FRANCISCO, May 7 (Reuters) - Software developer Rave on Thursday filed an antitrust lawsuit against , alleging that the iPhone maker ‌kicked Rave's app for shared viewing of video content off the Apple ‌App Store after introducing a competing product called SharePlay. Ontario, Canada-based Rave is demanding reinstatement into the ​App Store and "hundreds of millions of dollars" in damages, according to the complaint filed in U.S. federal court in New Jersey. Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment. Founded in 2013, Rave makes an app that lets users watch and discuss ‌video content together across Apple's ⁠iOS operating system, Android, Windows and Apple's Mac computers. The app remains available on Android and Windows. In the legal complaint, Rave ⁠alleges that Apple removed its app from the App Store in 2025 for what Apple called “dishonest or fraudulent activity.” Rave alleges that Apple's true reason for the removal was ​that ​Rave, which relied mostly on advertising revenue ​and therefore did not generate commission ‌revenue for Apple from in-app purchases, competed with a similar Apple product called SharePlay that Apple introduced in 2021.

article.title: Rave Sues Apple in Five Countries Over App Store Removal
article.subtitle: HAMILTON, Ontario, May 07, 2026--Rave Inc. ("Rave" or the "Company"), the developer and operator of the Rave app, a cross-platform co-viewing "super app...
article.author: Business Wire
article.content: )--Rave Inc. ("Rave" or the "Company"), the developer and operator of the Rave app, a cross-platform co-viewing "super app" with more than 225 million downloads, today announced that it has filed antitrust lawsuits against Apple in five countries: the United States, Canada, Brazil, the Netherlands and Russia. The lawsuits challenge Apple’s unilateral and anticompetitive decision to remove Rave from its App Store, thereby distorting competition, reducing consumer choice in co-viewing functionalities and increasing the costs to users of switching between iPhone and competing smartphone devices. Rave operates a social entertainment app that allows users in different locations to watch their favorite videos, movies, and TV shows together in real time across multiple operating systems and devices, including iOS, Android, Windows and macOS, and communicate through chat and voice. Rave alleges that this cross-platform experience threatened Apple’s closed iOS ecosystem, which Apple maintains by limiting the ways that iPhone users can interact with non-Apple devices or services.
article.summary: )--Rave Inc. article.title: Google Launches $100 Fitbit Air Without a Screen to Rival Whoop
article.subtitle: (Bloomberg) -- Alphabet Inc.’s Google on Thursday launched the Fitbit Air, a $100 screenless fitness band aimed squarely at taking on Whoop Inc. and other...
article.author: Samantha Kelly
article.content: (Bloomberg) -- Alphabet Inc.’s Google on Thursday launched the Fitbit Air, a $100 screenless fitness band aimed squarely at taking on Whoop Inc. and other makers of health and fitness wearables that forgo displays. The new device bears a striking resemblance to Whoop’s health tracker, featuring a soft fabric band with a battery and sensor pack underneath. One big difference is the business model: an upfront cost to buy the hardware and an optional $10 per month Google Health subscription. Whoop doesn’t charge for its hardware but instead has an annual subscription fee that begins at $200. The Fitbit Air may appeal to users seeking a simpler alternative to the Apple Watch — one with fewer distractions and notifications — or a cheaper option than rival health trackers. The popular Oura Ring health tracker, sold by Oura Health Oy, starts at $349, while the cheapest smartwatch from Apple Inc., the SE 3, is $249. Many of Google’s existing Fitbits cost over $100, while its Pixel Watch 4 is $349.
article.summary: (Bloomberg) -- Alphabet Inc.’s Google on Thursday launched the Fitbit Air, a $100 screenless fitness band aimed squarely at taking on Whoop Inc. and other makers of health and fitness wearables that forgo displays.

article.title: iPhone owners could get up to $95 from Apple settlement. Here's what to know.
article.subtitle: A proposed $250 million settlement would compensate millions of consumers who bought the iPhone 16, iPhone 15 Pro or iPhone 15 Pro Max.
article.author: Mary Cunningham
article.content: iPhone owners could receive up to $95 under a proposed $250 million settlement resolving claims that Apple misled customers about Siri's artificial intelligence features, according to a recent court filing. The class-action lawsuit, filed in federal court in the Northern District of California on behalf of U.S. consumers, claims that Apple deceived customers by marketing "Enhanced Siri features" that did not yet exist, leading them to buy iPhones based on false advertising. The plaintiffs are seeking a $250 million settlement payment, according to a recent court filing. If approved by a judge, it could rank among the largest settlements involving Apple. "Apple has reached a settlement to resolve claims related to the availability of two additional features," an Apple spokesperson said in an email to CBS News. "We resolved this matter to stay focused on doing what we do best, delivering the most innovative products and services to our users." Clarkson Law Firm, a public interest law firm that filed the original lawsuit against Apple, said in a statement on Wednesday that the settlement would provide "timely relief to tens of millions of consumers nationwide.
article.summary: iPhone owners could receive up to $95 under a proposed $250 million settlement resolving claims that Apple misled customers about Siri's artificial intelligence features, according to a recent court filing. The class-action lawsuit, filed in federal court in the Northern District of California on behalf of U.S.

article.content: Devendra Hardawar argues Apple (AAPL) is demonstrating discipline by moving slowly on AI while Microsoft (MSFT) accelerates spending. Apple delivered $111.2B in March-quarter revenue and $30.976B in Services—all-time records—without AI-driven capital outlays. Microsoft is boosting 2026 CapEx by $25B to $190B total as AI revenue hit $37B annually, but MSFT stock is down 14.11% YTD while AAPL is up 3.14%. The narrative around Apple and AI has flipped. A year ago, Apple’s restrained posture toward generative AI looked like a weakness. On the latest podcast, host Leo Laporte and panelists Devendra Hardawar, Micah Sargent, and Nicholas DeLeon argued the patient approach now looks like discipline, especially set against Microsoft’s accelerating capital outlays. As Hardawar explained, “Microsoft is pulling back on Copilot stuff. All these companies are kind of taking a second look at their AI investments. Apple can just sit tight and be slow about it.” and raised the entry price to $799, while warning Mac Mini and Mac Studio “may take several months to reach supply-demand balance.

Here are a few big reasons why MacBook Neo might be a powerful reason to buy By launching the MacBook Neo, Apple seems to be trying to drive additional sales beyond its flagship iPhone products. For years, many Apple observers questioned whether the company could design a low-cost laptop that fit the brand's premium standards. MacBook Neo seems to fit the bill -- and overall Mac sales are up because of it. According to the company's most recent earnings report on April 30, the iPhone still accounted for $56.

article.title: Arm beats expectations but shares fall on supply concerns for AI chip
article.subtitle: Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) reported quarterly earnings and guidance that surpassed Wall Street expectations, while highlighting strong customer demand for…
article.author: Fiona Craig
article.content: reported quarterly earnings and guidance that surpassed Wall Street expectations, while highlighting strong customer demand for its recently launched artificial intelligence-focused data center processor. Despite the upbeat results, U.S.-listed shares of the British semiconductor designer reversed sharp after-hours gains and fell 5% by 04:00 EST (08:00 GMT), after initially climbing as much as 13%. The decline followed comments during the company’s earnings call suggesting Arm has not yet secured enough supply capacity to satisfy an additional $1 billion in demand tied to its new AGI CPU. “Supply constraints led management to withhold raising its revenue forecast,” Raymond James analyst Simon Leopold wrote in a note. Arm is a major force in the semiconductor industry, with its chip architectures used by companies including Qualcomm, Apple, and Samsung in smartphone processors, as well as by cloud and hyperscale firms such as Amazon Web Services, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta. The company generates revenue by licensing its chip technology and collecting royalty fees on products built using its designs.
article.summary: reported quarterly earnings and guidance that surpassed Wall Street expectations, while highlighting strong customer demand for its recently launched artificial intelligence-focused data center processor. Despite the upbeat results, U.S.-listed shares of the British semiconductor designer reversed sharp after-hours gains and fell 5% by 04:00 EST (08:00 GMT), after initially climbing as much as 13%.

article.title: Broadcom shares slump as OpenAI’s $18B custom chip deal hits financing snag
article.subtitle: Investing.com -- Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) shares fell 4% Thursday afternoon following a report that its AI chip deal with OpenAI has encountered an $18 billion...
article.author: Louis Juricic
article.content: Investing.com -- Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) shares fell 4% Thursday afternoon following a report that its AI chip deal with OpenAI has encountered an $18 billion financing obstacle. The companies are negotiating an agreement for to finance the first phase of chip production, which would consume 1.3 GW of data center capacity and cost around $18 billion, according to The Information, citing an internal memo and two people involved in the talks. The negotiations have hit a potential snag, as Broadcom has said it would finance the first phase only if Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) agrees to buy roughly 40% of the chips, an OpenAI executive told colleagues in a memo last month. Under the proposed arrangement, would install the chips in its data centers and then rent them back to OpenAI. A purchase commitment from Microsoft would give Broadcom confidence it would get its money back, according to a person involved in the talks. However, Microsoft could choose not to buy OpenAI’s chips, which would change the financing terms for the project, the memo said.
article.summary: Investing.com -- Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) shares fell 4% Thursday afternoon following a report that its AI chip deal with OpenAI has encountered an $18 billion financing obstacle. The companies are negotiating an agreement for to finance the first phase of chip production, which would consume 1.3 GW of data center capacity and cost around $18 billion, according to The Information, citing an internal memo and two people involved in the talks.

article.title: Microsoft Reconsiders Energy Goal Amid AI Boom
article.subtitle: Data center growth pressures climate commitments
article.author: Moz Farooque ACCA
article.content: as the AI driven data center boom pushes energy demand to new levels. According to reports, the company is discussing whether to delay or potentially abandon its 2030 target of matching 100% of its hourly electricity usage with renewable energy purchases. The conversations are still ongoing and no final decision has been made, but the debate itself highlights how quickly AI infrastructure demands are reshaping energy priorities across Big Tech. The challenge comes down to scale. Microsoft has been adding nearly 1 gigawatt of data center capacity every 3 months as it races to support AI and cloud growth. That rapid expansion is increasing both electricity usage and emissions, despite major renewable energy investments. Since the launch of ChatGPT in late 2022, Microsoft's emissions have reportedly risen 23%, with the company itself pointing to AI and cloud expansion as major contributors. At the same time, the industry is increasingly leaning toward natural gas to meet near term power needs. Companies like Amazon (AMZN), Meta (META) and Google (GOOG) are also facing growing pressure as AI workloads surge.
article.summary: as the AI driven data center boom pushes energy demand to new levels. According to reports, the company is discussing whether to delay or potentially abandon its 2030 target of matching 100% of its hourly electricity usage with renewable energy purchases.

article.title: Why Microsoft Stock Is Up Today
article.subtitle: Microsoft Stock Surges as AI Discipline and Cloud Strength Fuel Investor Optimism
article.author: Nauman Khan
article.content: Microsoft stock jumped about 3% on Thursday as investors react to a mix of strong confidence signals across its core businesses and renewed optimism around its AI strategy. less by a single earnings release and more by a broader shift in sentiment toward Big Tech leadership in artificial intelligence and cloud computing. One key catalyst is investor approval of Microsoft's more disciplined AI approach. The company recently scaled back or canceled certain experimental AI features in its Xbox division, including parts of the Copilot gaming initiative. Markets interpreted this as a sign that Microsoft is tightening focus on high-impact, revenue-generating AI products rather than spreading resources too thin. At the same time, demand for Microsoft's enterprise AI tools remains strong. Its Microsoft 365 Copilot platform continues to expand paid adoption among businesses, reinforcing the company's ability to monetize AI at scale. That helps offset concerns about heavy capital spending on AI infrastructure. Broader market strength is also supporting the stock. Tech shares are leading gains across the S&P 500, with AI-related optimism lifting sentiment across megacap companies.
article.summary: Microsoft stock jumped about 3% on Thursday as investors react to a mix of strong confidence signals across its core businesses and renewed optimism around its AI strategy. less by a single earnings release and more by a broader shift in sentiment toward Big Tech leadership in artificial intelligence and cloud computing.

article.title: IBM Plummets 27% in 6 Months: Should You Reassess the Stock?
article.subtitle: IBM is down 27% in six months as Anthropic's Claude Code targets COBOL mainframes. Can hybrid cloud, HashiCorp and watsonx lift it?
article.author: Supriyo Bose
article.content: IBM has declined 27% over the past six months against the industry’s growth of 60.7% due to macroeconomic challenges and a sudden development in the artificial intelligence (AI) domain that threatens its core legacy businesses. The stock has lagged peers like AMZN. While Microsoft has declined 18.2%, Amazon has rallied 10.7% over this period. IBM’s downslide was primarily triggered by an announcement from AI firm Anthropic that its Claude Code tool can modernize legacy COBOL systems — a foundational programming language deeply embedded in IBM’s mainframe ecosystem. With Claude Code proposing to substantially automate code exploration, documentation, refactoring and security analysis, it threatened to reduce enterprises’ reliance on specialized legacy service providers like IBM, bringing its sustenance at stake. IBM is facing competition from Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure. Increasing pricing pressure is eroding margins, and profitability has trended down over the years, barring occasional spikes. Weaknesses in its traditional business and foreign exchange volatility remain significant concerns. High R&D cost is another concern.
article.summary: IBM has declined 27% over the past six months against the industry’s growth of 60.7% due to macroeconomic challenges and a sudden development in the artificial intelligence (AI) domain that threatens its core legacy businesses. The stock has lagged peers like AMZN.

In this role, he will continue to provide advice and counsel as the company enters the next phase of growth and success. "The technology industry and Microsoft ecosystem is being rewritten by AI, and the partners who will lead the next decade aren't the ones bolting AI onto existing playbooks — they're the ones rebuilding around it. article.author: Rich Duprey
article.content: ) is deploying roughly $185 billion in 2026 capital expenditures to fund AI data centers, custom TPUs, cloud infrastructure, and Gemini model training, while quietly downloading a 4GB Gemini Nano local AI model to Chrome users’ devices to shift inference workloads away from cloud servers. Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta collectively plan to spend $525 billion more on AI infrastructure this year, intensifying pressure for efficiency gains across the industry. Hyperscalers face mounting pressure to justify hundreds of billions in annual AI spending without durable monetization outside cloud subscriptions and advertising, prompting Google to shift computationally expensive AI inference tasks onto billions of consumer devices through Chrome to reduce data center and bandwidth costs. After two decades of patrolling the dark corners of suburbia as a police officer, Rich Duprey hung up his badge and gun to begin writing full time about stocks and investing. article.title: Where Will Chainlink (LINK) Be When the Next Bull Run Hits?
article.subtitle: This oft-overlooked cryptocurrency could have a bright future.
article.author: Leo Sun
article.content: This oft-overlooked cryptocurrency could have a bright future. , the native token of the Chainlink network, hit a record high of $52.99 on May 2021. But today, it trades at less than $10. Let's see why this hot cryptocurrency cooled off, and if it can bounce back once the next crypto bull market starts. Chainlink is a decentralized "oracle" network that connects blockchains to real-world data, including weather reports, stock prices, sports scores, and shipping information. It serves developer-oriented blockchains, such as , which need access to real-world data to support their decentralized applications. Chainlink's independent node operators fetch and deliver that all that data. Those operators are paid in LINK tokens, which they can stake (lock up) as collateral to earn interest-like rewards. But if they ever feed false data into Chainlink's network, those holdings can be confiscated, and their reputation scores can be reduced. Node operators with lower reputation scores are less likely to receive new requests, so the entire network runs on trust.
article.summary: This oft-overlooked cryptocurrency could have a bright future. , the native token of the Chainlink network, hit a record high of $52.99 on May 2021.

article.title: Why SiTime Rallied Today
article.subtitle: SiTime delivered impressive growth on its first quarter earnings report.
article.author: Billy Duberstein
article.content: SiTime delivered impressive growth on its first quarter earnings report. Shares of precision timing and oscillator chip specialist rallied on Thursday, up 25.6% as of 2:06 p.m. EDT. SiTime reported earnings yesterday, handily beating analyst estimates with huge growth. Like many other chipmakers, the agentic AI revolution has led to a step-change in the demand for SiTimes' products, and the momentum doesn't appear to be slowing anytime soon. In the first quarter, SiTime grew revenue 88.3% to $113.6 million, with adjusted (non-GAAP) EPS surging 443.8% to $1.44. Both figures blew past analyst expectations. Management also raised its full-year guidance to "at least" 80% growth. That fueled a big gain today, even after the stock had already surged 68.4% on the year. The stock is now up 123% in 2026. SiTime makes oscillators, clock ICs, and resonators, with the majority of revenue coming from oscillators. These chips help synchronize GPUs and CPUs during data processing, a synchronization that is crucial to the efficiency of an AI data center. Oscillators also help synchronize data traffic to and from high-speed networks, which is crucial to preventing data-transfer errors.
article.summary: SiTime delivered impressive growth on its first quarter earnings report. Shares of precision timing and oscillator chip specialist rallied on Thursday, up 25.6% as of 2:06 p.m.

article.title: Why Oklo Stock Is Climbing Higher This Week
article.subtitle: It's more than just company news that has investors excited.
article.author: Scott Levine
article.content: It's more than just company news that has investors excited. It's been a strong start to May for advanced nuclear reactor stock . Shares have been rising during this, the first full week of trading in May, thanks to the company's progress toward regulatory approval of its Aurora powerhouse. But it's not only company news that's driving investors to bid the stock higher. A nuclear energy peer's announcement from this week is also contributing to investors' interest in Oklo. Oklo stock has risen 2.8% from the end of trading last Friday through 2:42 p.m. ET today. Oklo informed investors yesterday that the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has approved the Principal Design Criteria (PDC) topical report for the Aurora powerhouse, which the company is developing in Idaho. According to Oklo, the approval means that the PDC topical report can now "be referenced in future applications and reduces the need to rereview established material" -- a development that should expedite the future processing of licensing applications for the Aurora powerhouse project. It's not only company news that has investors charged up this week.
article.summary: It's more than just company news that has investors excited. It's been a strong start to May for advanced nuclear reactor stock .

article.title: Why Anheuser-Busch Is Still a Buy Even Though People Are Drinking Less
article.subtitle: A focus on premium products and specialty drinks will keep the company relevant and growing.
article.author: Catie Hogan
article.content: A focus on premium products and specialty drinks will keep the company relevant and growing. According to the World Health Organization, global alcohol consumption decreased from 5.7 to 5.0 liters per capita between 2010 and 2022. This might not seem like much, but it is more than a 12% decrease worldwide in just 12 years. This trend has continued through the present day as global consumption fell another 2% in 2025. As health-conscious consumers purchase fewer alcoholic drinks, investors could sour on stocks such as , but it doesn't tell the entire story and isn't likely to happen. Let's have a look at why. Anheuser-Busch beat earnings again on Tuesday, May 5, with revenue increasing 5.8% year over year. Underlying earnings per share topped 20% in the first quarter of 2026. AB InBev is focusing on premium products, and this seems to be a bet that is paying off. The premiumization of products, combined with a focus on growing its "Beyond Beer" and no-alcohol beer categories, is the main driver behind the company's growth. In the Q1 2026 earnings release, AB InBev reported 27% increase in no-alcohol beer sales and 37% growth in Beyond Beer. focusing on premium beers.

article.author: Don Lair
article.content: A $39 Spirit flight to Dallas hit $124 by Sunday—the domestic market lost its fare floor when Spirit (ULCC competitor liquidated) shut down May 2. Delta Air Lines (DAL) and legacy carriers are raising Basic Economy fares because Spirit’s bankruptcy eliminated the “Spirit Effect” that forced discounts. American Airlines (AAL) faces new pricing power on its hubs, but consumer sentiment at 53.3 signals families won’t absorb a $200 summer vacation surcharge easily. United Airlines (UAL) will see fares jump $100 to $300 above normal on routes where Spirit held heavy capacity, especially Fort Lauderdale and Orlando. Frontier Group (ULCC) is running half-off promos to fill 1.8 million vanished seats, but 60,000 daily passengers will still shift to pricier legacy airlines. A round-trip ticket from Las Vegas to Dallas-Fort Worth cost $39 on Spirit Airlines the morning of May 1. By the afternoon of May 3, that same itinerary on the next-cheapest carrier was selling for $124. That’s a 217.9% jump, and it happened inside the 48-hour window after Spirit’s 3:00 AM ET shutdown on May 2. LAS-DFW is not an outlier.
article.summary: A $39 Spirit flight to Dallas hit $124 by Sunday—the domestic market lost its fare floor when Spirit (ULCC competitor liquidated) shut down May 2. Investors will be watching revenue acceleration, the company's outlook for the rest of the year and its backlog heading into 2027. The rally has already been dramatic. CoreWeave shares are up 84% this year and 229% since the company went public in March 2025, with the latest move fueled by renewed confidence in the AI trade and a quick series of deals with Meta, Anthropic and Jane Street Group. The shares fell 3.7% in early Thursday trading after rising 7.
article.summary: Thursday's earnings report with a powerful AI demand story behind it, but investors now want proof that the company can turn that demand into execution. The Livingston, New Jersey-based neo-cloud provider rents access to AI infrastructure built around the latest Nvidia ( ) chips, putting it directly in the path of rising compute needs from major AI spenders.

article.author: Dave Kovaleski
article.content: This stock got caught up in the SaaSpocalypse. One of the emerging trends in 2026 that has moved markets is the concerns over that focus on software-as-a-service (SaaS). Investors, particularly earlier in the year, began selling off these stocks for fear that advanced AI chatbots, notably Anthropic's Claude Cowork model, which rolled out in February, would replace and render useless the AI-driven software products and services that companies have developed in various niches. Terms like and SaaSmageddon were tossed about as these software stocks saw share prices sink rapidly. While some companies and products will likely be affected, the knee-jerk reaction cuts across pretty much all SaaS providers, even those that are not. They could even benefit from chatbots and AI models supporting their businesses. One of these SaaS stocks that should benefit in this new reality is . And with the sell-off, this under-the-radar AI stock is a screaming buy right now. AppLovin has a platform that helps companies monetize their apps and provide mobile advertising. It allows advertisers to find the right digital audience to download their apps and buy their products.

article.subtitle: SoftBank Corp has begun discussions with U.S. chip giant ‌Nvidia and Taiwanese contract manufacturer Foxconn ‌as it weighs plans to build "made-in-Japan...
article.author: Reuters
article.content: May 8 (Reuters) - SoftBank Corp has begun discussions with U.S. chip giant ‌ and Taiwanese contract manufacturer Foxconn ‌as it weighs plans to build "made-in-Japan" ​servers, the Nikkei newspaper reported on Friday. The Japanese telecom company wants to build a production system by initially assembling externally ‌sourced components by ⁠the end of the decade, before eventually taking charge of ⁠the entire server manufacturing process, Nikkei added. The unit will focus on high-performance servers ​capable of ​running advanced graphics ​processing units at ‌high speeds, the report also said. The project will be part of SoftBank's medium-term management plan, which, according to Nikkei, could be announced as soon as Monday. The ‌development comes after Masayoshi ​Son-led SoftBank Group poured ​in more than $30 ​billion in investments in ‌so far, for about ​an 11% ​stake, in an "all-in" bet that it will emerge as a winner in ​the battle ‌among developers of large language models. (Reporting ​by Shivangi Lahiri in Bengaluru; Editing ​by Diti Pujara)

article.title: Where Are Comcast and Charter's Internet Customers Going? Here.
article.subtitle: The cord-cutting movement is still well underway, but now, even cable TV's replacement cash cow is in trouble thanks to surprising competition.
article.author: James Brumley
article.content: The cord-cutting movement is still well underway, but now, even cable TV's replacement cash cow is in trouble thanks to surprising competition. The fact that streaming video is chipping away at cable television's customer base isn't anything new. That's been the case since 2013. But cable providers were able to count on their broadband internet business to offset this attrition. Now, however, it seems even high-speed internet is no longer a defensible business. Xfinity lost another 65,000 high-speed internet subscribers Spectrum shed 117,000 residential broadband customers in addition to a small handful of business customers. In both cases, this attrition extends what have become well-developed trends, too. Each company's internet customer count peaked in 2023, and each has since suffered losses in excess of 1 million subscribers. But if streaming requires a high-speed connection, where are all these broadband customers going?As it turns out, there's a relatively new option that's getting some serious traction. The top threat to the cable industry's high-speed internet business is no longer a rival cable name. It's wireless telecom names .
article.summary: The cord-cutting movement is still well underway, but now, even cable TV's replacement cash cow is in trouble thanks to surprising competition. The fact that streaming video is chipping away at cable television's customer base isn't anything new.

article.subtitle: US jobless claims increase less than expected amid low layoffs
article.author: Investing.com
article.content: Oil pares losses as Iran reviews peace deal, pushes new rules for Hormuz control Shorting this FX pair is the best hedge for war de-escalation: BofA Barclays sees the best setup for energy services in 20 years, names best stocks After soaring 9% yesterday, this AI-picked small cap is up 7%+ in premarket today WASHINGTON, May 7 (Reuters) - The number of Americans filing claims for unemployment benefits increased less than expected last week amid low layoffs that are helping to anchor the labor market. Initial claims for state unemployment benefits rose 10,000 to a seasonally adjusted 200,000 for the week ended May 2, the Labor Department said on Thursday. Economists polled by Reuters had forecast 205,000 claims for the latest week. The increase partially unwound the prior week’s decline. Government data on Tuesday showed there were 0.95 job openings for every unemployed person in March versus 0.91 in February, consistent with a stable labor market. Despite a raft of layoff announcements by big technology firms related to the adoption of artificial intelligence for some job roles, claims have remained below 230,000 this year.
